questions.stem: A company wants developers in a single AWS account to assume least-privilege roles. Developers occasionally need to delete S3 objects, but the security team wants to require MFA for any deletion. What is the MOST secure way to enforce this?
options: Use an S3 bucket ACL to restrict deletes
Attach an IAM policy to each developer that denies s3:DeleteObject
Add a Condition with aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent to the IAM policy granting s3:DeleteObject
Enable S3 Versioning on the bucket
questions.explanation: An IAM policy condition checking aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent grants the delete permission only when the caller authenticated with MFA, enforcing the requirement. A flat deny blocks deletes entirely. Versioning protects against loss but does not enforce MFA, and bucket ACLs cannot express MFA conditions.

questions.stem: A workload running on EC2 needs to read secrets from AWS Secrets Manager. What is the recommended way to grant access without storing long-lived credentials on the instance?
options: Attach an IAM instance profile (role) with permissions to read the secret
Store an IAM access key in user data
questions.explanation: An IAM instance profile delivers temporary, automatically rotated credentials to the EC2 instance via the metadata service, eliminating long-lived keys. Storing access keys in user data or AMIs exposes them and is insecure. Root credentials should never be used for workloads.

questions.stem: A company encrypts data in S3 using SSE-KMS with a customer managed key. They must be able to prove who decrypted objects and when, for compliance audits. Which service provides this record?
questions.explanation: CloudTrail logs KMS API calls such as Decrypt, recording the principal, time, and key used, satisfying the audit requirement. Config tracks resource configuration state, not individual API calls. Macie discovers sensitive data; Inspector assesses vulnerabilities.

questions.stem: An organization uses AWS Organizations and wants to guarantee that no account in a specific OU can disable CloudTrail, regardless of IAM permissions. What should they use?
options: A resource-based policy on the trail
A Service Control Policy (SCP) on the OU denying cloudtrail:StopLogging
An IAM permissions boundary on each user
questions.explanation: An SCP applied to the OU sets the maximum permissions for all principals in member accounts, so denying cloudtrail:StopLogging blocks it even for administrators. Permissions boundaries must be attached per principal and can be bypassed by account admins managing them. Resource policies and Config do not prevent the action.

questions.stem: A team needs to grant a partner in another AWS account temporary access to upload files to an S3 bucket without standing IAM credentials in your account. Which two approaches are appropriate? (Choose two)
options: Make the bucket public for writes
Generate short-lived pre-signed S3 URLs from an authorized service
Create an IAM user for the partner and share the access key
Create a cross-account IAM role the partner assumes via sts:AssumeRole
questions.explanation: A cross-account IAM role provides short-lived STS credentials, and programmatically generated pre-signed URLs grant time-limited upload access without standing credentials. Sharing an IAM user access key creates a long-lived secret in your account. Public write access is insecure and exposes the bucket to anyone.

questions.stem: A company wants to centrally manage encryption keys and automatically rotate them annually with no application changes. Which option meets this with the LEAST operational overhead?
options: Manually create a new key each year and re-encrypt data
Use a KMS customer managed key with automatic key rotation enabled
Store keys in an S3 bucket
questions.explanation: KMS customer managed keys support automatic annual rotation while keeping the same key ID, so applications need no changes and old data remains decryptable. Manual rotation is high overhead. SSE-S3 keys are managed by S3 but offer no customer control or audit of rotation policy as required.

questions.stem: An application must send sensitive data between two VPCs in the same Region without traversing the public internet. Which option is MOST appropriate and secure?
options: AWS Site-to-Site VPN over the internet
questions.explanation: VPC Peering connects two VPCs privately over the AWS network, and PrivateLink exposes specific services privately; both keep traffic off the public internet. Internet and NAT gateways route to the public internet. Site-to-Site VPN is for on-premises connectivity and adds unnecessary complexity here.

questions.stem: A security team wants to detect when an S3 bucket policy is changed to allow public access and be alerted automatically. Which combination achieves this? (Choose two)
options: Amazon EventBridge rule triggering an SNS notification on noncompliance
questions.explanation: The Config managed rule continuously evaluates buckets for public access, and an EventBridge rule on Config compliance change events can notify via SNS. Transfer Acceleration is for upload speed, Shield Advanced is for DDoS, and storage classes are unrelated to detecting public access.

questions.stem: A company must ensure all API calls to its AWS account are encrypted in transit and rejects any request not using TLS. For an S3 bucket, how can they enforce this?
options: Add a bucket policy that denies requests when aws:SecureTransport is false
Enable default encryption on the bucket
questions.explanation: A bucket policy with a Deny on aws:SecureTransport equals false rejects all non-HTTPS requests, enforcing TLS in transit. Default encryption protects data at rest, not in transit. Versioning is unrelated, and a VPC endpoint alone does not mandate TLS for all callers.

questions.stem: An auditor needs read-only visibility into all AWS resources across many accounts in an Organization, with a single point of management. What is the LEAST-overhead approach?
options: Use a cross-account role with the ReadOnlyAccess managed policy that the auditor assumes in each account
Create IAM users in every account
questions.explanation: A standardized cross-account role with ReadOnlyAccess lets the auditor assume it in each account using one identity, centralizing management. Per-account IAM users multiply credentials and overhead. Sharing root credentials is insecure, and disabling accounts defeats the purpose.

questions.stem: A web application running on EC2 in private subnets must download OS patches from the internet but must not be reachable from the internet. Which solution is correct?
options: Place the instances in a public subnet
Route outbound traffic through a NAT Gateway in a public subnet
Use an Egress-Only Internet Gateway for IPv4
Attach an Internet Gateway and assign public IPs
questions.explanation: A NAT Gateway in a public subnet allows instances in private subnets to initiate outbound IPv4 connections while remaining unreachable from the internet. Public IPs and public subnets expose instances. Egress-Only Internet Gateways are for IPv6 only.

questions.stem: A company stores PII in DynamoDB and wants to control which fields each microservice can read. Which approach provides fine-grained, attribute-level access control?
options: Encrypt the whole table with KMS
Use IAM policies with dynamodb:Attributes condition keys and projection
questions.explanation: IAM policies support fine-grained access control using condition keys like dynamodb:Attributes to restrict which attributes a principal can access. Whole-table encryption protects at rest but not per-field authorization. Streams capture changes, and VPC endpoints control network path, not attributes.

questions.stem: An organization wants to scan EC2 instances and container images for software vulnerabilities and unintended network exposure automatically. Which service is purpose-built for this?
questions.explanation: Amazon Inspector continuously scans EC2 instances and ECR container images for software vulnerabilities and network reachability issues. GuardDuty detects threats from logs, Config tracks configuration compliance, and Macie classifies sensitive data in S3.

questions.stem: A company needs to grant a Lambda function permission to write to a specific DynamoDB table and nothing else. What is the BEST practice?
options: Use an IAM user access key in environment variables
Attach AdministratorAccess to the function role
Create an execution role with a policy scoped to dynamodb:PutItem on that table ARN
questions.explanation: An execution role scoped to the specific action and table ARN follows least privilege. AdministratorAccess grants far too much. Embedding access keys is insecure and unnecessary because Lambda uses its execution role. Public write access is never appropriate.

questions.stem: A regulated workload requires that encryption keys never leave a FIPS 140-2 Level 3 validated hardware boundary and that the company has exclusive control. Which service meets this?
options: AWS KMS with AWS managed keys
questions.explanation: AWS CloudHSM provides dedicated, single-tenant HSMs validated to FIPS 140-2 Level 3 under the customer's exclusive control. KMS AWS managed keys are multi-tenant and not customer-exclusive. SSE-S3 and Secrets Manager do not provide dedicated HSM control.

questions.stem: A company wants to allow mobile app users to authenticate with Google and then access AWS resources with temporary, limited credentials. Which service should they use?
options: AWS IAM Identity Center for workforce SSO
questions.explanation: Cognito Identity Pools federate external identity providers like Google and vend temporary AWS credentials scoped by IAM roles, ideal for mobile users. Directory Service is for managed AD. IAM users are not for large user bases. IAM Identity Center is for workforce, not consumer app, sign-in.

questions.stem: A team must ensure that EBS volumes created in their account are always encrypted, without relying on developers to remember. What is the simplest enforcement?
options: Use an SCP that denies ec2:RunInstances
Enable EBS encryption by default at the account/Region level
questions.explanation: Enabling EBS encryption by default ensures all newly created volumes and their snapshots are encrypted automatically in that Region. Manual audits are reactive and error-prone. Denying RunInstances blocks all launches. Encrypting only snapshots does not protect new volumes.

questions.stem: An application uses an Application Load Balancer and must offload TLS while protecting against common web exploits like SQL injection. Which addition provides this protection?
options: Associate AWS WAF with the ALB
Use a Network Load Balancer instead
questions.explanation: AWS WAF integrates with the ALB to inspect HTTP(S) requests and block SQL injection and other layer-7 attacks using managed and custom rules. Shield Standard covers DDoS, not application exploits. An NLB operates at layer 4 and cannot inspect HTTP. Flow Logs only record metadata.

questions.stem: A company centralizes secrets and wants applications to retrieve database credentials that rotate automatically every 30 days, integrating with RDS. Which service is MOST appropriate?
options: AWS Secrets Manager with rotation enabled
Using KMS to store the password
questions.explanation: Secrets Manager natively rotates RDS credentials on a schedule using a managed rotation Lambda, meeting the 30-day requirement with minimal effort. Standard Parameter Store does not rotate secrets automatically. S3, hardcoding, and KMS (which stores keys, not arbitrary secrets) are inappropriate.

questions.stem: A security team detects unusual API activity and wants continuous, intelligent threat detection across CloudTrail, VPC Flow Logs, and DNS logs with no agents. Which service fits?
questions.explanation: GuardDuty analyzes CloudTrail, VPC Flow Logs, and DNS logs using machine learning to detect threats, requiring no agents. Config evaluates configuration compliance. Inspector scans for software vulnerabilities. Trusted Advisor gives best-practice checks, not threat detection.

questions.stem: An ecommerce site experiences unpredictable traffic spikes. The team wants the web tier to scale automatically and remain highly available across multiple AZs with minimal management. Which architecture is BEST?
options: A single large EC2 instance with a static Elastic IP
On-demand instances launched manually during spikes
EC2 instances in one AZ behind a Network Load Balancer
An Auto Scaling group of EC2 instances across multiple AZs behind an Application Load Balancer
questions.explanation: An Auto Scaling group spanning multiple AZs behind an ALB adds and removes capacity automatically and survives an AZ failure, providing high availability and elasticity. A single instance is a single point of failure. One-AZ designs are not highly available, and manual launches do not scale automatically.

questions.stem: A relational database must survive the failure of an entire Availability Zone with automatic failover and synchronous replication. Which RDS configuration provides this?
options: RDS with a larger instance class
questions.explanation: RDS Multi-AZ maintains a synchronous standby in another AZ and fails over automatically if the primary AZ fails. Single-AZ has no standby. Cross-Region read replicas are asynchronous and used for DR or read scaling, not automatic same-Region failover. A larger class does not add availability.

questions.stem: A company needs a globally distributed, multi-Region database for an online game leaderboard with single-digit millisecond reads and writes in each Region. Which service fits BEST?
questions.explanation: DynamoDB Global Tables provide multi-Region, active-active replication with low-latency local reads and writes, ideal for a global leaderboard. RDS Multi-AZ is single-Region. Redshift is an analytics warehouse. A single ElastiCache node is neither durable nor multi-Region.

questions.stem: An application decouples order processing using a queue. Order processing must occur exactly in the sequence received and avoid duplicates. Which AWS service and feature is correct?
questions.explanation: An SQS FIFO queue preserves message order and provides exactly-once processing within a message group, meeting both requirements. Standard SQS offers best-effort ordering and at-least-once delivery. SNS standard topics do not guarantee order. Firehose is for streaming delivery to stores, not ordered queue processing.

questions.stem: A microservice must remain available even if a downstream service is temporarily down. The team wants to buffer requests and process them when the downstream recovers. Which pattern is BEST?
options: Direct synchronous HTTP calls between services
Place an SQS queue between the services to decouple them
Use a larger instance for the downstream service
Increase the timeout on the caller
questions.explanation: Putting an SQS queue between services decouples them so the producer keeps working and messages are processed when the consumer recovers, improving resilience. Synchronous calls couple availability. Larger timeouts or instances do not provide buffering or fault isolation.

questions.stem: A read-heavy application puts high load on its RDS database from repeated identical queries and growing read traffic. Which two changes reduce database read load most effectively? (Choose two)
options: Add RDS read replicas to serve read queries
Reduce the database backup retention period
Switch the database to RDS Single-AZ
Add an in-memory cache with Amazon ElastiCache
questions.explanation: ElastiCache offloads repeated reads from memory, and RDS read replicas distribute read traffic across additional instances; together they reduce load on the primary. Switching to Single-AZ lowers availability without helping reads. Backup retention changes do not affect read performance.

questions.stem: A static website with global users must load quickly worldwide and remain available even during origin outages for cached content. Which combination is BEST?
options: EC2 web servers in one Region
S3 in one Region accessed directly
Amazon S3 origin with Amazon CloudFront distribution
questions.explanation: CloudFront caches S3 content at global edge locations, reducing latency for worldwide users and serving cached content even if the origin is briefly unavailable. Direct S3 access has higher latency for distant users. EC2 in one Region is not global, and EBS cannot be shared globally.

questions.stem: An application needs a shared file system mountable by hundreds of Linux EC2 instances across multiple AZs concurrently, scaling automatically. Which service fits?
questions.explanation: Amazon EFS is an elastic, fully managed NFS file system that multiple EC2 instances across AZs can mount concurrently and that scales automatically. EBS attaches to limited instances and is single-AZ. S3 is object storage, not a POSIX file system. Instance store is ephemeral and local.

questions.stem: A company wants near-zero RPO and RTO for a critical workload by running full production capacity in two Regions simultaneously with traffic routed to both. Which DR strategy is this?
questions.explanation: Multi-site active/active runs full capacity in multiple Regions serving live traffic, giving the lowest RPO and RTO. Backup and restore and pilot light have higher recovery times. Warm standby runs a scaled-down copy that must scale up on failover, so it is not full active/active.

questions.stem: An application uses Lambda functions triggered by events. The team wants resilient, asynchronous fan-out so one event notifies multiple independent consumers. Which service should publish the events?
options: Amazon SNS topic with multiple subscriptions
questions.explanation: An SNS topic supports fan-out, delivering each published message to multiple subscribers such as several queues or Lambda functions independently. A single SQS queue delivers each message to one consumer. Direct Lambda chaining couples functions, and EBS is block storage.

questions.stem: A company runs a workflow with many sequential and parallel steps that must be reliably coordinated with retries and error handling, visualized as a state machine. Which service is BEST?
questions.explanation: AWS Step Functions orchestrates multi-step workflows as a state machine with built-in retries, error handling, and parallel branches. SQS only queues messages. Batch runs batch compute jobs. EventBridge Scheduler triggers tasks on a schedule but does not coordinate complex workflows.

questions.stem: An Aurora database serves a reporting workload that occasionally overwhelms the primary, affecting writes. The team wants to offload reads and scale read capacity easily. What should they add?
options: Aurora Replicas behind the reader endpoint
questions.explanation: Aurora Replicas share the cluster storage and serve read traffic via the reader endpoint, offloading reads from the writer and scaling read capacity. A larger primary does not isolate reads from writes. A separate Single-AZ instance is unrelated, and a NAT Gateway is for network egress.

questions.stem: A company wants its REST API to remain available and automatically scale to handle bursts, while keeping the team free of server management. Which fronting service is BEST?
options: A single EC2 instance running the API
Amazon API Gateway integrated with Lambda
questions.explanation: API Gateway is a fully managed, automatically scaling entry point that integrates with Lambda for serverless backends, removing server management. A single EC2 instance is not highly available or auto-scaling. On-premises hardware lacks elasticity. Auto Scaling alone still requires managing instances.

questions.stem: A company stores session state for a horizontally scaled web fleet so users keep their sessions even when a server is replaced. Which approach provides low-latency, shared session storage?
options: Use Amazon ElastiCache for Redis as a shared session store
Use sticky sessions only with no external store
Store sessions on each instance's local disk
questions.explanation: ElastiCache for Redis provides fast, shared, in-memory session storage that survives instance replacement and supports horizontal scaling. Local disk and EBS are tied to individual instances and lost on replacement. Sticky sessions alone break when the target instance is removed.

questions.stem: A latency-sensitive global application uses TCP and needs static anycast IP addresses with instant failover and traffic routed to the nearest healthy Regional endpoint. Which service is BEST?
questions.explanation: Global Accelerator provides static anycast IPs and routes traffic over the AWS backbone to the nearest healthy endpoint with fast failover, ideal for non-HTTP, latency-sensitive TCP/UDP traffic. CloudFront optimizes HTTP content delivery. Route 53 failover depends on DNS TTLs. A single ALB is Regional.

questions.stem: A company needs to ingest and process a high-volume, continuous stream of clickstream data in real time, with multiple consumers reading the same stream. Which service is BEST?
questions.explanation: Kinesis Data Streams handles high-throughput real-time streaming and lets multiple consumers independently read the same records, suiting clickstream analytics. SQS is a queue where each message is typically consumed once. SNS is pub/sub messaging, and Glue is batch ETL, not real-time streaming ingestion.

questions.stem: An application's batch jobs can tolerate interruption and restart. The team wants resilient, cost-aware compute that automatically replaces interrupted capacity. Which approach is BEST? (Choose two)
options: Configure the Auto Scaling group to span multiple instance types and AZs
Use EC2 Spot Instances in an Auto Scaling group with a mixed instances policy
questions.explanation: Spot Instances in an Auto Scaling group lower cost for interruptible work, and a mixed-instances policy across multiple types and AZs improves capacity availability and resilience when Spot is reclaimed. A single On-Demand instance is neither resilient nor cost-optimized. Dedicated Hosts add cost without improving resilience for interruptible work.

questions.stem: A company stores 500 TB of infrequently accessed log archives that must be retrievable within minutes a few times a year. Which S3 storage class is MOST cost-effective?
questions.explanation: S3 Glacier Flexible Retrieval offers very low storage cost for rarely accessed archives, with expedited retrievals available in minutes, fitting the access pattern. S3 Standard and Standard-IA cost more for rarely accessed data. One Zone-IA stores in a single AZ and is not suited for durable long-term archives.

questions.stem: An application has unpredictable access patterns across millions of S3 objects, and the team wants to minimize cost without performance impact or manual tiering. Which storage class is BEST?
questions.explanation: S3 Intelligent-Tiering automatically moves objects between access tiers based on usage, optimizing cost for unpredictable patterns with no retrieval fees for frequent/infrequent tiers and no performance impact. Standard does not optimize cost. Deep Archive has long retrieval times. One Zone-IA reduces durability.

questions.stem: A team wants to reduce EC2 costs for a steady 24/7 baseline workload running for the next three years, with flexibility to change instance family within a compute type. Which purchasing option is BEST?
options: Standard Reserved Instances locked to one instance type
questions.explanation: A 3-year Compute Savings Plan offers deep discounts for steady usage and applies flexibly across instance families, sizes, Regions, and even Fargate/Lambda. On-Demand is most expensive for steady use. Spot is for interruptible work. Standard RIs give discounts but lock to a specific instance family, reducing flexibility.

questions.stem: A company wants to identify which AWS services and accounts are driving cost increases and to set alerts when spending exceeds thresholds. Which tools should they use? (Choose two)
questions.explanation: Cost Explorer visualizes and analyzes spending trends by service and account, and AWS Budgets triggers alerts when costs exceed defined thresholds. CloudWatch Logs Insights queries logs and Config tracks resource configuration; neither provides cost alerting or trend analysis.

questions.stem: A data lake stores files in S3, and analysts want to run ad hoc SQL queries directly on the data without provisioning servers, paying only per query. Which service fits?
questions.explanation: Athena is a serverless query service that runs SQL directly on S3 data and charges per amount of data scanned, ideal for ad hoc queries with no infrastructure. A Redshift provisioned cluster and EMR require managing and paying for running clusters. RDS is for transactional databases, not querying S3 files.

questions.stem: A company runs nightly EC2-based ETL that takes about two hours. They want to minimize compute cost and can tolerate occasional reruns if interrupted. Which option is MOST cost-effective?
questions.explanation: Spot Instances provide the deepest discounts and suit interruptible, restartable batch jobs like nightly ETL, minimizing cost. On-Demand is more expensive. Reserved Instances suit steady 24/7 usage, not occasional nightly runs. Dedicated Hosts are the most expensive option, used for licensing or compliance.

questions.stem: An S3 bucket holds data that should transition to cheaper storage after 30 days and be deleted after 365 days automatically. What is the simplest way to implement this?
options: Write a Lambda function on a schedule to move and delete objects
Configure an S3 Lifecycle policy with transition and expiration rules
questions.explanation: An S3 Lifecycle policy automates transitions to cheaper classes after a set age and expires objects after a defined period with no code. A scheduled Lambda is unnecessary custom work. Replication copies data rather than tiering it, and manual moves are error-prone and high overhead.

questions.stem: A company transfers 10 PB of data to AWS, and their network would take months. They need a secure, physical bulk transfer appliance. Which service is appropriate?
options: Direct upload to S3 over the public internet
questions.explanation: The AWS Snow Family ships physical devices for petabyte-scale offline data transfer when network transfer is impractical, with encryption in transit. DataSync and direct uploads depend on network bandwidth and would take too long. Storage Gateway provides hybrid storage access, not bulk one-time migration of 10 PB.

questions.stem: An EBS-backed database needs consistent high IOPS performance for a latency-sensitive transactional workload. Which EBS volume type should be used?
options: Provisioned IOPS SSD (io2 or io2 Block Express)
questions.explanation: Provisioned IOPS SSD (io2/io2 Block Express) delivers consistent, high IOPS and low latency for demanding transactional databases. gp2 offers baseline burstable performance not guaranteed for sustained high IOPS. st1 and sc1 are HDD types optimized for throughput or cold storage, unsuitable for low-latency transactional I/O.

questions.stem: A company wants to reduce data transfer costs and latency for repeated requests to its origin while serving global users. Which two changes help achieve this? (Choose two)
options: Enable compression for text-based responses at the edge
Use Amazon CloudFront to cache content at the edge
Serve all content directly from EC2 in one Region
questions.explanation: CloudFront caches content at edge locations to cut origin load and data-transfer-out costs and lower latency, while edge compression reduces the bytes transferred for text responses. Serving from one Region raises transfer cost and latency for distant users. A larger instance does not reduce data transfer cost.

questions.stem: A team running containers wants to eliminate EC2 capacity management and pay only for the vCPU and memory their tasks consume. Which launch type should they choose?
options: Amazon ECS on EC2 launch type
questions.explanation: AWS Fargate runs containers serverlessly, removing EC2 capacity management and charging only for the vCPU and memory requested by tasks. The ECS EC2 launch type and self-managed Kubernetes still require managing instances. Reserved Instances are an EC2 pricing model, not a serverless container option.

questions.stem: A petabyte-scale analytics warehouse must run complex SQL aggregations across structured data with columnar storage and the ability to query S3 data directly. Which service fits?
questions.explanation: Redshift is a columnar data warehouse built for large-scale analytical SQL, and Redshift Spectrum queries data directly in S3 without loading it. DynamoDB is a NoSQL key-value store. RDS for MySQL is row-based OLTP, not optimized for petabyte analytics. ElastiCache is an in-memory cache.

questions.stem: A company wants to reduce NAT Gateway data processing charges for EC2 instances that frequently access S3 and DynamoDB within the same Region. What is the MOST cost-effective change?
options: Create S3 and DynamoDB Gateway VPC endpoints
questions.explanation: Gateway VPC endpoints for S3 and DynamoDB route traffic privately within AWS at no per-GB cost, bypassing the NAT Gateway and avoiding its data processing charges. Adding NAT Gateways increases cost. Internet Gateways and public subnets expose instances and still incur transfer costs for these services via NAT otherwise.

questions.stem: A company has many idle and underutilized EC2 instances and wants recommendations to right-size them to reduce cost. Which service provides this analysis?
questions.explanation: AWS Compute Optimizer analyzes utilization metrics and recommends optimal instance types and sizes to reduce cost and improve performance. Inspector scans for vulnerabilities, Config tracks configuration state, and CloudWatch alarms alert on metrics but do not recommend right-sizing.

questions.stem: A Windows-based application requires a fully managed, high-performance shared file system supporting SMB and NTFS with Active Directory integration. Which service is correct?
options: Amazon FSx for Windows File Server
questions.explanation: FSx for Windows File Server provides a fully managed Windows-native file system with SMB, NTFS, and Active Directory integration. EFS is NFS for Linux. S3 is object storage without SMB. FSx for Lustre targets high-performance computing workloads, not Windows file shares.

questions.stem: A company wants to migrate a production database to AWS with minimal downtime, continuously replicating changes until cutover. Which service should they use?
options: AWS Database Migration Service (DMS) with ongoing replication
questions.explanation: DMS performs an initial load and then continuously replicates ongoing changes (CDC), enabling near-zero-downtime cutover. Snowball is for bulk offline transfer, not live replication. Transfer Acceleration speeds S3 uploads, and a manual dump/import requires extended downtime during the migration window.

questions.stem: An operations team must be alerted automatically when average EC2 CPU utilization exceeds 80% for five minutes. Which AWS service provides this monitoring and alerting?
questions.explanation: CloudWatch alarms evaluate metrics like CPUUtilization against thresholds over a period and trigger notifications or actions, meeting the requirement. CloudTrail records API calls, not performance metrics. Config tracks configuration compliance, and X-Ray traces application requests, not infrastructure metrics.

questions.stem: A team wants to centralize logs from many EC2 instances and run queries to troubleshoot issues. Which combination achieves this with the LEAST operational overhead?
options: Store logs only on each instance
Install the CloudWatch agent to ship logs to CloudWatch Logs and query with Logs Insights
Copy log files to S3 manually each day
questions.explanation: The CloudWatch agent streams instance logs to CloudWatch Logs, and Logs Insights provides fast interactive querying, centralizing troubleshooting with little management. Manual S3 copies and emailing logs are high overhead and slow. Keeping logs only on instances loses them when instances are replaced.

questions.stem: A company must record a complete history of configuration changes to all AWS resources and evaluate them against compliance rules over time. Which service should they enable?
questions.explanation: AWS Config records resource configuration history and continuously evaluates resources against rules for compliance. CloudTrail logs API activity but not full configuration state over time. CloudWatch monitors metrics and logs. Trusted Advisor gives point-in-time best-practice checks, not configuration history.

questions.stem: A team wants to provision identical, repeatable infrastructure across multiple environments using version-controlled templates managed by AWS natively. Which service is BEST?
options: Editing resources with the CLI ad hoc
Manually creating resources in the console
questions.explanation: CloudFormation defines infrastructure as code in templates that can be version-controlled and deployed repeatably across environments. Console and ad hoc CLI changes are not repeatable or tracked. Run Command executes operational commands on instances but does not provision and manage full infrastructure stacks.

questions.stem: An application's distributed requests are slow, and the team needs to trace requests across microservices to find bottlenecks. Which service provides end-to-end tracing?
questions.explanation: AWS X-Ray traces requests as they flow through distributed microservices, producing service maps and latency analysis to pinpoint bottlenecks. CloudWatch Logs stores log data, CloudTrail records API calls, and Config tracks configuration; none provide distributed request tracing.

questions.stem: A company wants to patch operating systems on hundreds of EC2 instances on a schedule and report compliance, without SSH access to each host. Which service should they use?
questions.explanation: Systems Manager Patch Manager automates patching on a schedule and reports patch compliance across fleets via the SSM agent, avoiding per-host SSH. Inspector scans for vulnerabilities but does not apply patches. CloudFormation provisions infrastructure. Manual SSH scripts are high overhead and error-prone.

questions.stem: A team needs to automatically respond to an EC2 instance failing its health check by replacing it without human intervention. Which configuration provides this self-healing?
options: An Auto Scaling group with health checks that terminates and replaces unhealthy instances
questions.explanation: An Auto Scaling group monitors instance health and automatically terminates and launches replacements for unhealthy instances, providing self-healing. A dashboard only visualizes metrics. Manual monitoring is not automatic. Termination protection actually prevents instances from being terminated, blocking replacement.

questions.stem: A company wants notifications routed to different targets based on the content of events from many AWS services, with flexible filtering rules. Which service is BEST?
questions.explanation: EventBridge ingests events from AWS services and custom sources, applies content-based filtering rules, and routes matching events to many targets. SQS only queues messages without rule-based routing. CloudTrail records API activity, and CloudWatch dashboards visualize metrics rather than route events.

questions.stem: An operations team wants to deploy application updates to an Auto Scaling group while keeping the previous version running until the new one is verified, minimizing risk. Which deployment strategy is BEST?
options: Deleting the Auto Scaling group and recreating it
Manually stopping all instances during deployment
In-place update of all instances at once
questions.explanation: A blue/green deployment runs the new version alongside the old and shifts traffic only after verification, enabling quick rollback and minimal risk. Updating all instances at once risks a full outage if the release is faulty. Stopping instances or recreating the group causes downtime.

questions.stem: A company needs operational visibility and wants to detect anomalies in metrics automatically without manually setting static thresholds. Which CloudWatch capability helps?
questions.explanation: CloudWatch Anomaly Detection applies machine learning to learn normal metric patterns and alert on deviations without manual static thresholds. Static threshold alarms require manually chosen limits. CloudTrail Insights detects unusual API activity, not metric anomalies, and Config rules evaluate configuration compliance.

questions.stem: A team wants to automate operational responses, such as restarting a service when a CloudWatch alarm fires, using predefined runbooks. Which service provides this automation? (Choose two)
options: Amazon CloudWatch alarm actions invoking SSM or Lambda
questions.explanation: Systems Manager Automation runbooks define repeatable remediation steps, and CloudWatch alarm actions can invoke SSM Automation or Lambda to trigger them automatically. Macie classifies sensitive data and Shield mitigates DDoS; neither automates operational remediation.

questions.stem: A company must store CloudTrail logs immutably for seven years to meet compliance, preventing deletion or modification. Which configuration is BEST?
options: Store logs in an S3 bucket with S3 Object Lock in Compliance mode and a retention period
Store logs on an EBS volume
Keep logs only in CloudWatch Logs with a 30-day retention
questions.explanation: S3 Object Lock in Compliance mode enforces write-once-read-many retention so logs cannot be deleted or altered, even by root, for the specified period. EBS volumes are not immutable and tie to instances. A 30-day CloudWatch retention fails the seven-year requirement, and DynamoDB is not designed for immutable log archives.
